desc: Florence is a compact Renaissance powerhouse where headline galleries, frescoed churches, and artisan streets all sit within a walkable historic core. It stays attractive year-round, but crowd pressure is strongest in spring and early autumn; midsummer is hotter with more midday slowdown, while winter gives easier museum access and lower lodging pressure.
hls: Renaissance landmarks: Duomo, Uffizi, Accademia, Ponte Vecchio
Highly walkable center with dense cultural coverage
Excellent Tuscan dining and wine-bar scene in Oltrarno
Strong base for Siena, Chianti, Lucca, and Bologna day trips
Classic skyline viewpoints from Piazzale Michelangelo
High-speed rail links to Rome, Milan, Venice, and Naples

todo.name: Duomo complex (Cathedral, Dome, Baptistery)
todo.desc: Book combined tickets to climb Brunelleschi's dome and visit the cathedral museum.

todo.name: Uffizi Galleries
todo.desc: See Botticelli, Leonardo, and Caravaggio in one of Europe's essential collections.

todo.name: Accademia Gallery
todo.desc: Visit Michelangelo's David and unfinished Prisoners sculptures.

todo.name: Ponte Vecchio and Arno river walk
todo.desc: Cross Florence's iconic bridge and continue along both riverbanks at sunset.

todo.name: Palazzo Pitti & Boboli Gardens
todo.desc: Combine palace galleries with landscaped gardens behind the Oltrarno district.

todo.name: Mercato Centrale
todo.desc: Try Tuscan specialties and browse produce, cheeses, and regional deli stalls.

todo.name: Piazzale Michelangelo viewpoint
todo.desc: Walk or bus up for classic skyline views over the Duomo and river.

todo.name: Chianti or Siena day trip
todo.desc: Use Florence as a base for Tuscan vineyards, hill towns, and medieval centers.

wifi.note: Good in most central stays and coworking hubs; historic buildings can have weak signal in upper floors or rear rooms, so request confirmed in-room speeds for work trips.

prac.fltNote: Nonstop Gatwick–Florence service is limited/seasonal, so many UK travelers compare Florence (FLR) with Pisa (PSA) or Bologna (BLQ) and continue by train. Return fares commonly cluster around ~£60–£130 off-peak, ~£130–£220 in shoulder demand, and ~£220+ in summer-holiday or major-event weeks.
prac.visa: Italy is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can usually stay visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period.
alerts: Accademia and Uffizi timed-entry inventory can sell out days ahead during spring/autumn peaks.
Historic-center pavements and limited shade make July–August afternoons feel significantly hotter than shoulder months.
Major fair weeks (e.g., Pitti events) can compress central hotel availability and push rates up.

sweet: Late April to June and September to mid-October are best for long walking days, active museum schedules, and stronger value than top-peak weeks.
